title: Advertising Manager
keyDrivers: This role sits at the top of a function where AI is compressing the middle. The core task — planning and preparing advertising material — shows 30.8% automation across 2,700+ observed conversations, meaning the execution work that managers oversee is being increasingly automated. The role scores low because it combines AI-exposed output work with a declining market for traditional advertising management: demand for these roles was already being squeezed by programmatic advertising before AI added a second wave of pressure.
taskIntro: Not all tasks are affected equally. Knowing which ones AI handles well, and which still need a human, is how to focus skill-building.

risks.body: The execution layer of advertising management — media planning, copy approval, campaign trafficking, and performance reporting — is being absorbed by AI-powered platforms, reducing the headcount needed to run campaigns of the same scale. eMarketer's 2024 data shows that 91% of digital display advertising is now bought programmatically, eliminating much of the manual media buying coordination that justified mid-level advertising management roles [1]. Kantar's 2024 Media Reactions report found that advertiser budgets are shifting away from agency-managed creative production toward in-house AI tools, compressing the scope of work that flows through advertising managers [2].
risks.statLabel: of digital display ads bought programmatically, removing manual coordination
risks.statSourceTitle: US Programmatic Digital Display Ad Spending 2024

opportunities.body: Brand strategy, agency relationships, and the judgment calls about creative direction and campaign risk remain human-accountable work. HubSpot's 2024 State of Marketing report found that 74% of marketing leaders say AI has increased the volume of campaigns their teams run, expanding the strategic coordination load rather than replacing it [1]. Advertising managers who shift from overseeing production to owning brand standards and agency performance are expanding their scope as AI handles the execution underneath them.
opportunities.statLabel: of marketing leaders say AI increased their campaign volume

howToAdapt.alreadyIn: Build fluency in AI-assisted campaign tools now so the work you oversee is produced faster and at lower cost — this matters because budget pressure is ongoing in this sector. Over six months, shift your value proposition toward the parts of the role that AI handles worst: client strategy, cross-functional coordination, and the judgment calls about creative direction that require context no tool has [2]. Managers who can operate with leaner teams by using AI effectively are more valuable than those who manage large teams producing output that AI could generate [1].
howToAdapt.thinkingOf: With only 2,100 annual job openings and negative projected growth, this is a competitive field to enter. Build a portfolio that demonstrates measurable campaign outcomes — not just campaign management experience — since employers are increasingly focused on ROI rather than headcount [3]. A path through digital marketing or content strategy roles provides a stronger foundation than traditional advertising agency work, which is facing the steeper structural decline [1].

quotes.quote: The advertising managers who are navigating this environment well are those who have redefined their value around strategy and client relationships, while using AI tools to dramatically reduce the cost and time of production work their teams previously spent most of their time on.
quotes.attribution: Future of Jobs Report 2025, World Economic Forum

quotes.quote: Marketing and advertising is one of the functions where generative AI creates the most economic value — which means the practitioners who adapt are those who shift from managing content production to managing AI-assisted systems and directing strategic decisions.
quotes.attribution: The economic potential of generative AI, McKinsey Global Institute

quotes.quote: Programmatic platforms now manage 91% of U.S. digital display ad spending automatically, but the remaining strategic decisions about brand positioning, creative direction, and cross-channel budget allocation are where advertising managers justify their roles.
quotes.attribution: US Programmatic Digital Display Ad Spending 2024, eMarketer

quotes.quote: The advertising managers most in demand combine creative judgment with data literacy — they can evaluate both the AI-generated campaign variants and the brand implications of running them.

taskData.full: Plan and prepare advertising and promotional material to increase sales of products or services, working with customers, company officials, sales departments, and advertising agencies.
Plan and execute advertising policies and strategies for organizations.
Devise or evaluate methods and procedures for collecting data, such as surveys, opinion polls, and questionnaires.
Monitor and analyze sales promotion results to determine cost effectiveness of promotion campaigns.
Confer with department heads or staff to discuss topics such as contracts, selection of advertising media, or product to be advertised.
Coordinate activities of departments, such as sales, graphic arts, media, finance, and research.
Inspect layouts and advertising copy, and edit scripts, audio, video, and other promotional material for adherence to specifications.
Manage sales team, including setting goals, providing incentives, and evaluating employee performance.
Direct, motivate, and monitor the mobilization of a campaign team to advance campaign goals.
Analyze the effectiveness of marketing tactics or channels.

careerCluster.title: Marketing Specialist
careerCluster.fit: Entry-level research and communications role that builds the analytical and market knowledge foundation.
steps: Build a portfolio of research summaries and data visualizations
Learn SQL and a BI tool (Tableau or Power BI)
Develop subject matter expertise in a specific industry vertical

careerCluster.title: Search Marketing Strategist
careerCluster.fit: Digital marketing specialist role; search/SEO skills complement content strategy and brand management.
steps: Earn Google Analytics and Google Ads certifications
Build a portfolio showing SEO-driven content performance
Learn paid media fundamentals via Meta Blueprint

careerCluster.title: Public Relations Specialist
careerCluster.fit: Moving to PR Specialist is a step down from Advertising Manager in seniority, but a lateral shift in function — the focus moves from paid and promotional media to earned media and stakeholder communication.
steps: Build media pitching and press release experience
Develop media contact relationships in a target industry
Learn crisis communications and spokesperson coaching

careerCluster.title: Writer
careerCluster.fit: Ad & Promotions Managers often oversee writers and can step back into producing their own work — it is a step down in management scope but a move toward creative autonomy and individual craft.
steps: Build an independent bylined portfolio
Develop copywriting clients or publication relationships
Write across commercial formats (ad copy, content, editorial)

careerCluster.title: Editor
careerCluster.fit: Ad Managers who specialize in the content quality side of their role can step into editing — it is a step down in management scope, shifting from campaign oversight to editorial judgment and content standards.
steps: Build a substantive editing portfolio (3+ publications)
Study AP Style and editorial decision-making
Develop experience managing a content calendar or publication

careerCluster.title: Marketing Manager
careerCluster.fit: Advertising & Promotions Manager is a natural step toward Marketing Manager — it is a step up that expands scope from campaign execution to full marketing strategy, brand management, and digital channel integration.
steps: Build experience managing digital channel budgets
Add brand and product marketing to campaign work
Manage a cross-functional marketing team

careerCluster.title: Advertising Sales Agents
careerCluster.fit: Advertising Sales Agents who develop platform expertise and campaign analytics experience can transition into the Advertising & Promotions Manager role — the sales background provides client relationship skills, while the new role adds creative direction and campaign budget ownership.
steps: Build Google Ads and Meta Ads campaign management experience
Develop campaign analytics and reporting skills
Move from selling ad products to planning and executing campaigns

emergingCareers.description: Leads automated media buying strategy across DSPs and real-time bidding platforms. Manages AI-driven audience targeting, bid optimization, and campaign attribution at scale.
emergingCareers.core_tools: The Trade Desk, Google DV360, LiveRamp
emergingCareers.stat_text: Programmatic advertising now accounts for 91% of all digital display ad spend in the U.S., making programmatic expertise the dominant skill requirement for senior ad roles.
emergingCareers.stat_title: US Programmatic Digital Display Ad Spending 2024
emergingCareers.fit: Campaign planning and media mix experience transfers; the shift is from managing human creative and media teams to overseeing AI-driven bidding systems and first-party data strategy.
steps: Complete The Trade Desk Edge Academy certification
Build a programmatic campaign using Google DV360 with documented performance results
Learn clean room and privacy-safe targeting concepts via LiveRamp's resource library

emergingCareers.description: Owns full-funnel customer acquisition strategy using AI-powered experimentation, predictive lifetime value modeling, and paid channel optimization. Bridges brand strategy and performance marketing.
emergingCareers.stat_text: Growth marketing director roles commanding $180k+ grew 19% in 2024, with AI-fluency listed as a required skill in 67% of senior postings per LinkedIn data.
emergingCareers.fit: Cross-channel campaign ownership and budget management experience are directly applicable; the new orientation is toward data-driven experimentation loops and predictive modeling rather than campaign creative oversight.
steps: Complete Reforge's Growth Series program
Build a full-funnel attribution model using Northbeam or Triple Whale
Earn a data analytics certification via Google or Coursera

emergingCareers.description: Directs creative strategy for campaigns where AI tools generate assets, copy variations, and personalized ad content at scale. Sets creative standards, tests AI output against human benchmarks, and governs brand consistency.
emergingCareers.stat_text: Creative director job postings requiring generative AI tool experience grew 78% in 2024, with agencies and brands racing to formalize AI creative governance roles.
emergingCareers.fit: Campaign creative oversight and brand standards experience are the qualifying assets; the new skill is directing AI tools as a creative production layer rather than managing human designers and copywriters.
steps: Build a campaign using Adobe Firefly and document the creative direction process
Develop a brand AI usage policy covering tone, imagery, and disclosure standards
Complete Adobe's Generative AI for Creative Professionals course

sources.name: eMarketer
sources.title: US Programmatic Digital Display Ad Spending 2024
